title: How to Install Xiaomi MiClaw
description: Step-by-step guide to install Xiaomi MiClaw on your Android phone. Setup, features, and tips for getting the most out of your AI agent.
body_html:
What is Xiaomi MiClaw and Why You Need It

You might find yourself in a situation where your hands are covered in flour while baking, or you are driving and need to reply to an urgent message. This is where a hands-free [voice control](voice-control-android) tool becomes indispensable. Xiaomi MiClaw is an advanced system designed to act as a smart helper directly on your mobile device, making hands-off operation simple and reliable.

this tool acts as an active [AI agent](agentic-ai-phone-explained) that can execute tasks across multiple applications without manual input. Unlike older systems that only search the web, this setup understands the context of your screen and can take actions on your behalf. It bridges the gap between basic voice commands and true automation.

By setting up this tool, you gain access to deep system integration that goes beyond standard options like [Google AI](gemini-intelligence-complete-guide). It is built to run lightweight local models alongside cloud services, giving you the best of both worlds. Whether you want to draft emails, manage smart home devices, or organize your calendar, this software provides a practical starting point.

For users who want to explore advanced tools without being locked into one ecosystem, this program offers a great entry point. It works in tandem with modern frameworks, allowing your device to interpret complex instructions. If you are tired of tapping through multiple menus just to complete a simple task, this tool is the solution you need.

Device Requirements and Hardware Compatibility

Before you begin the installation, you must ensure your hardware can handle the processing demands of this modern software. Because this system relies on local machine learning models to process commands, older hardware will struggle or fail to run it. you need a device running at least Android 13, though Android 14 is highly recommended for optimal performance.

In terms of processing power, your phone should have a modern processor with a dedicated neural processing unit. Devices equipped with Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 or newer, or equivalent MediaTek Dimensity chipsets, show the best results. You also need a minimum of 8GB of RAM, as the local model needs to stay resident in system memory to respond quickly to your prompts.

Storage space is another critical factor to consider before downloading. The core package is relatively small, but the offline language models can take up anywhere from 2GB to 6GB of space. Make sure you have at least 10GB of free internal storage to avoid installation errors or system slowdowns during operation.

While the tool is optimized for Xiaomi's HyperOS, it can run on other Android distributions with some modifications. However, non-Xiaomi users might need to install additional driver packages to get full hardware acceleration. Checking these specifications beforehand will save you time and prevent system crashes later on.

Step-by-Step Installation Process

Installing the tool requires a few more steps than a standard app store download, as it is not always available in every regional store. First, you need to enable installation from unknown sources in your Android security settings. This allows you to install the official APK package directly from the developer portal.

Once your settings are updated, download the latest version of the installation package from the authorized repository. Open your file manager, locate the downloaded file, and tap it to start the process. If your system warns you about security risks, confirm that you trust the source to proceed with the package installation.

After the main application is installed, do not launch it immediately. You must download the corresponding model weights file, which contains the brain of the system. Move this model file into the designated folder within your device's internal storage, usually located under the application's data directory.

failing to place the model files in the correct directory is the most common reason for startup failures. Double-check that the file path matches the instructions provided in the setup guide. Once both the application and the model files are in place, you can safely restart your phone to apply the changes.

First-Time Configuration and Permissions

Once the app is open, the configuration phase is where you truly define how the system interacts with your phone. The initial startup wizard will guide you through a series of system prompts. You will need to grant accessibility services permission, which is necessary for the tool to read your screen and perform actions.

Next, you must set up the voice activation features. The application will ask you to record your voice several times to train the local detection model. This ensures that the system only responds to your specific voice, preventing accidental activations from background noise or other people speaking nearby.

You will also need to link your preferred online accounts if you want to use advanced external models. For example, you can connect your [Claude AI](claude-ai-login-android) account to handle complex reasoning tasks that go beyond the capabilities of the local offline model. This hybrid approach ensures you get fast local responses for simple tasks and deep analysis for harder queries.

Finally, review the battery optimization settings on your Android device. By default, Android aggressively closes background apps to save battery life. You must exclude this application from battery optimization to ensure it remains active and ready to assist you whenever you speak the wake word.

Using MiClaw for Daily Tasks and Automation

Using the tool in your daily life is where the power of [voice control](voice-control-android) truly shines. Instead of typing out long search queries or manually opening five different apps, you can speak a single natural sentence. The system analyzes your intent, plans the necessary steps, and executes them in sequence.

For instance, you can ask the system to find a specific photo from your last trip, edit the brightness, and send it to a contact on WhatsApp. The local [AI assistant](ai-agent-vs-traditional-apps) handles these steps by interacting with your apps just like a human would, saving you valuable time and effort during busy workdays.

the system excels at scheduling and productivity tasks. You can dictate complex calendar events, set location-based reminders, and draft quick replies to incoming emails. Because the system runs locally, these basic commands are executed almost instantly, without the latency associated with cloud-only services.

You can also create custom automation chains for your daily routines. For example, saying "start my morning" can trigger the system to read your calendar, play your favorite playlist, and open your navigation app with your commute route. This level of customization makes your smartphone feel truly personalized and responsive to your habits.

MiClaw vs FoneClaw: Which Should You Choose?

When deciding between MiClaw and FoneClaw, the choice often comes down to your specific hardware setup and how much control you want over your data. MiClaw is deeply integrated into the Xiaomi ecosystem, making it a fantastic choice if you own a compatible Xiaomi or Redmi device. However, its performance on other brands can be unpredictable.

On the other hand, FoneClaw is built by an independent startup as a versatile, cross-platform option. It supports various models, including standard Android voice services, but is designed to run smoothly on a wider range of Android hardware. This makes FoneClaw a more flexible choice if you use devices from multiple manufacturers or plan to switch brands in the future.

Additionally, FoneClaw offers a more straightforward setup process for general users. While MiClaw requires manual model file placement and technical configurations, FoneClaw simplifies these steps through an intuitive interface. This makes it easier for non-technical users to access advanced automation features without getting bogged down in system files.

Ultimately, both platforms offer outstanding capabilities for modern voice automation. If you want a deeply integrated system tailored for Xiaomi hardware, MiClaw is an excellent option. If you prefer a flexible, user-friendly platform that works across different brands, FoneClaw is the ideal companion for your mobile device.

faq.question: Is Xiaomi MiClaw free to use on Android?
faq.answer: Yes, the core application and local models are free to download and use. However, if you decide to connect premium third-party cloud services for advanced reasoning tasks, you may need to pay for API usage or subscriptions associated with those specific external providers.

faq.question: Can I install MiClaw on a Samsung phone?
faq.answer: Yes, you can install it on non-Xiaomi devices, but you may experience limited performance. Since the software is optimized for HyperOS, some system-level features might not work correctly on Samsung's One UI without manual workarounds and additional driver packages.

faq.question: Does MiClaw require an active internet connection to work?
faq.answer: No, the system can perform basic tasks offline using its local language models. However, for complex queries that require web searches or advanced reasoning, the application will need an active internet connection to access cloud services.

faq.question: Is FoneClaw associated with Xiaomi or MiClaw?
faq.answer: No, FoneClaw is an independent startup. While FoneClaw supports Xiaomi's models, it is a separate platform designed to offer a more flexible and user-friendly automation experience across a wider variety of Android devices and brands.

faq.question: How do I update the local models for MiClaw?
faq.answer: To update the models, you must download the latest model weights file from the official repository. Then, replace the old file in your device's internal storage directory and restart the application to apply the updates.

faq.question: What is FoneClaw?
faq.answer: FoneClaw is an Android AI phone assistant that turns voice commands into supported phone actions such as device checks, message summaries, settings changes, screenshots, navigation, and other everyday workflows.

summary: FoneClaw is an Android AI phone assistant for supported phone actions, not just a chatbot.
